text:
COMPREHENSIVE SOLID TUMOR NEXT-GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT
Molecular Pathology / Oncogenomics Laboratory

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
PATIENT & SPECIMEN INFORMATION
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
Patient Name:         Margaret Tran
Date of Birth:        [on file]
Medical Record #:     [on file]
Ordering Physician:   Thoracic Medical Oncology
Diagnosis (submitted): Lung adenocarcinoma, right lower lobe
Specimen ID:          SP-2024-XXXXXX
Specimen Type:        Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) core needle biopsy
Procedure:            CT-guided transthoracic needle biopsy, right lower lobe mass
Tumor Site:           Right lower lobe, lung primary
Tissue Processing:    FFPE block; H&E-confirmed tumor content 60% (reviewed by molecular pathologist)
Specimen Adequacy:    ADEQUATE for next-generation sequencing
Analysis Type:        Tumor-normal paired analysis (matched peripheral blood leukocytes as germline reference)
Panel:                Comprehensive Solid Tumor Panel вҖ” 523 genes (coding exons + select introns for fusion detection)
Sequencing Platform:  Illumina NovaSeq 6000
Mean Tumor Coverage:  648x
Mean Normal Coverage: 312x
Date Received:        [on file]
Date Reported:        [on file]
Accession #:          NGS-XXXX-XXXXXX

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
METHODOLOGY
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
DNA was extracted from macrodissected FFPE tumor tissue and matched peripheral blood. Library preparation was performed using a hybridization capture-based approach targeting 523 cancer-relevant genes, including full coding exons, hotspot regions, and selected intronic regions for fusion partner detection. Paired-end sequencing was performed on the Illumina NovaSeq 6000 platform. Variant calling was performed using a validated bioinformatics pipeline with filters for strand bias, base quality, and mapping quality. Somatic variants were confirmed by subtraction of matched germline variants. Copy number analysis was performed using a log2 ratio method normalized to matched germline. Fusion detection utilized split-read and discordant read-pair analysis. Microsatellite instability (MSI) was assessed by interrogating 114 microsatellite loci. Tumor mutational burden (TMB) was calculated as the number of somatic nonsynonymous mutations per megabase of sequenced coding territory. Germline analysis covered 84 hereditary cancer predisposition genes per American College of Medical Genetics (ACMG) guidelines.

Analytical sensitivity: >98% for substitutions and small indels with allele frequency вүҘ5% at adequate depth; >95% for copy number alterations with вүҘ4-fold amplification.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
SOMAT IC ALTERATIONS вҖ” RESULTS SUMMARY
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

TIER I вҖ” ALTERATION WITH STRONG CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE (FDA-approved therapy or professional guideline)

TIER II вҖ” ALTERATION WITH POTENTIAL CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE (clinical investigation context)

в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
GERMLINE ANALYSIS
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
Germline analysis of 84 hereditary cancer predisposition genes (BRCA1, BRCA2, MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2, APC, ATM, PALB2, CHEK2, and others per ACMG secondary findings list) was performed using the matched peripheral blood leukocyte sample.

Result: NO PATHOGENIC OR LIKELY PATHOGENIC GERMLINE VARIANTS IDENTIFIED in cancer predisposition genes.

Variants of uncertain significance (VUS): One heterozygous VUS identified in ATM (c.3161C>T, p.T1054M); clinical significance uncertain; germline genetic counseling offered as standard of care.

Note: This germline analysis is provided for informational purposes. Confirmatory germline testing through a CLIA-certified clinical genetics laboratory is recommended if germline counseling is pursued.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
INTERPRETATION
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
The most clinically significant finding in this tumor specimen is an EGFR p.L858R missense mutation in exon 21 (VAF 34.2%). This is one of the two most common activating mutations in EGFR-mutant non-small cell lung cancer (the other being exon 19 deletions), representing approximately 40-45% of all EGFR-mutant NSCLC cases. The L858R substitution results in constitutive activation of the EGFR kinase domain through stabilization of the active conformation, driving downstream oncogenic signaling through the RAS/MAPK, PI3K/AKT, and STAT pathways.

Importantly, this specimen was obtained prior to any systemic therapy exposure. The absence of EGFR T790M at baseline is consistent with a de novo treatment-naive tumor and does not preclude the development of T790M as an acquired resistance mechanism following EGFR TKI therapy.

The co-occurring TP53 p.R175H mutation (VAF 41.8%) is a well-characterized hotspot gain-of-function mutation in the DNA-binding domain of p53. TP53 co-mutations are identified in approximately 40-60% of EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinomas. Published data suggest that TP53 co-mutations вҖ” particularly exon 5 hotspot mutations including R175H вҖ” may be associated with somewhat shorter progression-free survival on first- and second-generation EGFR TKIs, though the prognostic impact with third-generation inhibitors such as osimertinib remains an area of active investigation.

Heterozygous deletion of CDKN2A (encoding p16/INK4A and p14/ARF) is a common secondary alteration in lung adenocarcinoma and may contribute to cell cycle dysregulation but is not currently independently actionable in this context.

The following negative findings are particularly noteworthy:
- No KRAS mutation: KRAS and EGFR mutations are biologically mutually exclusive in lung adenocarcinoma and the absence of KRAS is concordant with the EGFR-mutant genotype.
- ALK, ROS1, RET fusions: Negative, consistent with EGFR-mutant biology (co-occurrence of EGFR with these fusions is exceedingly rare).
- STK11 and KEAP1: Wild type. The absence of STK11/KEAP1 co-mutations is favorable, as these alterations are associated with resistance to immunotherapy and potentially with altered biology in EGFR-mutant tumors.
- MET exon 14 skipping and MET amplification: Not detected at baseline, relevant for future resistance assessment.
- TMB low (6.2 mut/Mb) and MSS: Consistent with typical EGFR-mutant adenocarcinoma biology. This tumor is unlikely to benefit from immune checkpoint inhibition as primary therapy, consistent with published data (e.g., IMpower150 subset analyses and CheckMate trials demonstrating limited benefit of immunotherapy monotherapy in EGFR-mutant NSCLC).
